title: mentha sylvestris subsp. kotschyana
intro: Mentha sylvestris subsp. kotschyana, also known as Kotschy's wild mint, is a subspecies of mint native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and Europe. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid. The health benefits of Kotschy's wild mint include its potential to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and headaches, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce stress. Kotschy's wild mint is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be combined with other herbs to create a range of medicinal remedies.
